IOGC 2026 National Seminar

Explore Forest Park

One of the largest urban parks in the United States — right here in Saint Louis. Enjoy our excursion recommendations and find your way back to the hotel when you're ready.

Forest Park Map
📍 Forest Park, Saint Louis, Missouri  |  1,371 acres  |  Free admission
Excursion Recommendations

Four world-class destinations — all free to enter, all inside Forest Park.

🦁
Saint Louis Zoo
One of the top-ranked zoos in the nation, home to 14,000+ animals across 90 acres. Walk through exhibits featuring big cats, sea lions, penguins, primates, and much more. Stroller and wheelchair friendly.
Free Admission
🎨
Saint Louis Art Museum
Over 34,000 works spanning 5,000 years of history — from ancient Egypt to modern masters. Perched on Art Hill with one of the best views in the park. A truly stunning building inside and out.
Free Admission
📜
Missouri History Museum
Explore the story of St. Louis through immersive exhibits on the 1904 World's Fair, Lewis & Clark, Civil Rights history, and more. A fascinating deep-dive into the Gateway City's past.
Free Admission
🔬
Saint Louis Science Center
Hundreds of hands-on exhibits covering space, technology, nature, and the human body. Connected to the James S. McDonnell Planetarium — don't miss the live shows. Great for all ages.
Free Admission
Good to Know
💡
Tips from the Saint Louis Locals
  • All four attractions are completely free to enter — no tickets needed.
  • The Zoo is best visited in the morning before crowds build. Allow at least 2 hours.
  • The Art Museum sits on Art Hill — the view from the front steps is one of the best in the park.
  • The History Museum is a quieter, relaxed experience — perfect if you want something low-key.
  • The Science Center connects directly to the Planetarium — check show times when you arrive.
  • Free parking is available near each attraction throughout the park.
Directions Back to the Hotel

Forest Park → Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel

  1. Head north out of Forest Park on Skinker Blvd
  2. Merge onto I-70 West toward St. Louis Lambert Airport
  3. Continue on I-70 West for approximately 10 miles
  4. Take the Natural Bridge Rd exit and turn right
  5. The Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel will be on your right at 9801 Natural Bridge Rd
🚗  Approx. 13 miles ⏱️  Approx. 20–30 minutes
🏨
Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel 9801 Natural Bridge Rd, St. Louis, MO 63134

Gateway Arch · Old Courthouse · Riverboat Cruise — three iconic landmarks steps apart along the Mississippi River.

How the Locations Connect

All three sites sit along the same north-south corridor — a comfortable 10–12 minute walk end to end.

📍 Gateway Arch National Park · Old Courthouse · Riverboats at the Gateway Arch · Downtown St. Louis
Excursion Recommendations

Three iconic landmarks — all within the same National Park grounds along the riverfront.

🏛️
Old Courthouse
One of St. Louis' oldest landmarks and the site of the historic Dred Scott freedom trial. Beautifully restored with exhibits on the city's legal and civil rights history. Located just west of the Arch — about a 5–7 minute walk away.
Free Admission
🌉
Gateway Arch
The iconic 630-foot stainless steel arch at the heart of Gateway Arch National Park. Ride the unique tram to the top for sweeping views of St. Louis and the Mississippi River. The museum at the base tells the story of westward expansion.
Tram ticket required
⛵
Riverboat Cruise
Board a classic riverboat right at the Mississippi River docks, just east of the Arch. Narrated sightseeing cruises offer stunning views of the St. Louis skyline and the Arch from the water — an unforgettable photo opportunity.
Ticket required
Good to Know
💡
Tips from the Saint Louis Locals
  • All three sites are within easy walking distance — you can do all three in a single outing.
  • Start at the Old Courthouse, walk through the Arch grounds, then head to the riverfront for the cruise.
  • The Arch tram tickets can sell out — book online in advance at archpark.org if possible.
  • The Riverboat Cruise dock is right at the base of the Arch on the river side — you can't miss it.
  • Best photo spot: stand on the Arch grounds and look back toward downtown for the classic skyline shot.
Directions Back to the Hotel

Gateway Arch area → Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel

  1. Head west from the Arch on Washington Ave or Market St into downtown
  2. Merge onto I-70 West toward St. Louis Lambert Airport
  3. Continue on I-70 West for approximately 12 miles
  4. Take the Natural Bridge Rd exit and turn right
  5. The Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel will be on your right at 9801 Natural Bridge Rd
🚗  Approx. 14 miles ⏱️  Approx. 20–30 minutes
🏨
Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel 9801 Natural Bridge Rd, St. Louis, MO 63134

A historic landmark reimagined as a full entertainment complex — aquarium, Ferris wheel, ropes course, and more, all under one roof.

Union Station Overview
📍 St. Louis Union Station · 1820 Market St, St. Louis, MO 63103 · All attractions in one complex
How the Attractions Connect

Everything is inside the same entertainment complex — no transportation needed between stops.

Excursion Recommendations

Five attractions packed into one historic entertainment complex.

🐠
St. Louis Aquarium
One of the newest attractions in downtown St. Louis, featuring thousands of aquatic animals across beautifully themed exhibits. Located inside Union Station near the main entrance — the anchor of the whole complex.
Ticket required
🎡
St. Louis Wheel
A stunning 200-foot Ferris wheel overlooking the Union Station lake and the downtown St. Louis skyline. Located just outside near the lake — one of the best photo spots in the city, especially at dusk.
Ticket required
🧗
Union Station Ropes Course
A thrilling multi-level ropes course with bridges, obstacles, and challenges for all skill levels. Located inside the attractions complex right next to the Mirror Maze — great for groups and team fun.
Ticket required
🪞
Union Station Mirror Maze
A disorienting and fun maze of mirrors and LED lights — surprisingly tricky and always good for laughs. Located right next to the Ropes Course in the same attractions area. Perfect for any age.
Ticket required
🚂
Selfie Express
A train-themed photo experience perfect for groups, families, and anyone wanting a memorable keepsake. Located steps from the Aquarium entrance — a quick and fun stop that is easy to add to any visit.
Ticket required
Good to Know
💡
Tips from the Saint Louis Locals
  • All five attractions are inside the same complex — once you arrive, everything is a short walk away.
  • Consider a combo ticket if your group wants to do the Aquarium, Ropes Course, and Mirror Maze together — it saves money.
  • The St. Louis Wheel is especially magical at sunset — try to time it for the late afternoon.
  • The Aquarium is the biggest time commitment — allow 1.5 to 2 hours for a full visit.
  • The historic Union Station hall itself is worth a look — beautiful Victorian Gothic architecture dating back to 1894.
Directions Back to the Hotel

Union Station → Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel

  1. Exit Union Station onto Market Street heading east
  2. Merge onto I-64 East / I-40 East, then follow signs to I-70 West toward the airport
  3. Continue on I-70 West for approximately 10 miles
  4. Take the Natural Bridge Rd exit and turn right
  5. The Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel will be on your right at 9801 Natural Bridge Rd
🚗  Approx. 11 miles ⏱️  Approx. 18–25 minutes
🏨
Renaissance St. Louis Airport Hotel 9801 Natural Bridge Rd, St. Louis, MO 63134
